LC
Then & Now
Deceased · 2011
Larry
Cate
SP4 / Door Gunner  ·  Recon · 11F · Door Gunner (from late July 1971)
Platoon
Cat (Wild Cat) Platoon
In-Country Service
1970 – 1971
Home of Record
Powell, Knox Co., TN
Character of Service
Honorable
Decorations & Awards
Combat Infantry Badge Vietnam Service Medal w/2 Stars Vietnam Campaign Medal w/60 Device National Defense Service Medal
Share a Memory
Service Timeline
1970 – 1971

Reconstructed from unit records, the McGrew field calendar, accounts from fellow soldiers, and photographs taken by SGT Marvin Dale Miller. Compiled by the Miller family, 2024–2025. Larry's exact arrival and departure dates are under ongoing research.

1970
Arrival
Arrives In-Country — Cat Platoon
Larry Cate arrived in Vietnam and was assigned to Cat (Wild Cat) Platoon, Delta Company, 2nd Battalion, 8th Cavalry, 1st Cavalry Division (Airmobile). Exact arrival date is under research.
20 Apr 1971
Combat Action
Contact North of FSB Fanning — Three KIA
Delta Company made contact with an NVA base camp north of Route 1, east of Gia Rai Mountain. Cat Platoon's patrol spotted enemy soldiers bathing in a creek. Range Platoon, crossing to the north bank, walked into a fortified bunker complex. CPL James Cardwell, CPL Danny Drinkard, and CPL Joseph Hall were killed. Larry was present with Cat Platoon during this action.
⚠ Source Notice — Multiple Accounts Exist
The summary above draws from written depositions of Capt. Bill Neal and SSgt Stan Dillon, supplemented by accounts from other unit members. Details of sequence, positioning, and circumstance vary between accounts. No single account should be read as a definitive record.
24 Apr 1971
Incident
Huey Crash at FSB Fontaine — Three Killed
A Huey departing FSB Fontaine crashed shortly after takeoff, killing the pilot, co-pilot, and Battalion Armor SP4 Richard Colburn. Larry was present at FSB Fontaine at the time of the crash.
May 1971
Combat Action
Capture of a Chieu Hoi
Cat Platoon was involved in the capture of a Chieu Hoi — a Viet Cong or NVA soldier who surrendered under the South Vietnamese amnesty program. Exact date and circumstances are under research.
Late Jul 1971
Role Change
Transitions to Door Gunner
Larry moved from ground operations to a door gunner position on a UH-1 Huey during unit rotations in late July 1971. His Warrant Officer designation is under research.
1971
Discharge
Returns Home — Honorable Discharge
Larry Cate returned home to Powell, Tennessee following his tour. Exact departure date is under research.
Photographs
3 Images

All photographs of Larry Cate in this archive were taken by his fellow Cat Platoon soldier SGT Marvin Dale Miller, presumed before August 1971 when Larry transitioned to a door gunner role.

Did You Know Larry, or Serve Alongside Larry?

We are actively collecting photographs, letters, and memories for this profile. Submissions are reviewed before publication. If you served with Larry — or are a family member with materials — we'd be grateful to hear from you.

Submit Materials

1st Cavalry Division (Airmobile) · Vietnam 1965–1972
